extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: One long, central plateau where the signal stays clearly lower than the baseline level (a “valley” in the middle of the series).  
R2: On both sides of this valley (at the beginning and the end), the signal is roughly flat and close to the same baseline level.  
R3: At one side of the valley (before or after it), there is a single pronounced, narrow peak that rises well above the baseline.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: One long, central plateau where the signal stays clearly higher than the baseline level (a “bump” in the middle of the series).  
R2: On both sides of this bump (at the beginning and the end), the signal is roughly flat and close to the same baseline level.  
R3: No additional sharp peaks or deep dips appear outside the central bump.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: One long, central segment where the signal is clearly lower (valley) or higher (bump) than the baseline, similar in length to the central region of classes 0 and 1.  
R2: On one side of this central segment, there is a very sharp, deep dip that goes much lower than any other part of the series.  
R3: On the other side of the central segment, there is a roughly flat part at baseline level, without large peaks.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series has one long plateau that is clearly lower than the values at the beginning and end (a “valley” in the middle).
R2: The values before and after this middle valley are roughly equal and form short, relatively flat plateaus.
R3: There is at most one prominent spike (short, sharp peak) near one of the transitions between plateau and valley.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series has one long plateau that is clearly higher than the values at the beginning and end (a “hill” in the middle).
R2: The values before and after this middle hill are roughly equal and form short, relatively flat plateaus.
R3: There are no strong extra spikes or dips inside the high plateau or near its edges; the shape is essentially a single clean block.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series changes level twice inside the central region, so that you see two distinct low or high excursions (two separated “valleys” or “dips” / “spikes”) instead of just one block.
R2: Between these two excursions, the signal briefly returns close to its original baseline level.
R3: At least one of the excursions is sharp and narrow compared with the wide hill/valley seen in classes 0 and 1.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series has two plateau levels: one high and one low, with a long central segment at the low level (middle part significantly below the outer parts).  
R2: The level before the low plateau and after it is approximately the same (start and end at similar medium values).  
R3: There is a single, short, sharp peak above the high level near one end of the series (a spike higher than any other point).
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series has two plateau levels: one high and one baseline, with a long central segment at the high level (middle part significantly above the outer parts).  
R2: The level before the high plateau and after it is approximately the same (start and end at similar lower values).  
R3: No sharp spike or dip deviates strongly from these two plateau levels (the shape is roughly a single “block” without extra extremes).
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series has three plateau levels: baseline, opposite plateau (low if baseline is high, or high if baseline is low), then a return close to the baseline.  
R2: Within or near the return segment, there is an additional short, very sharp opposite extreme (a narrow spike or dip much more extreme than the main plateau difference).  
R3: Overall, there are at least two distinct extreme segments of opposite sign relative to the baseline (e.g., a long low region plus a very sharp deeper dip, or a long high region plus a very sharp higher spike).

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series has one wide plateau whose value is clearly lower than both the earlier and later parts of the series.  
R2: After this low plateau, the series returns to a higher baseline and shows a single sharp peak above that baseline.  
R3: Before the low plateau begins and after the sharp peak ends, the values stay roughly at the same higher baseline level.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series has one wide plateau whose value is clearly higher than both the earlier and later parts of the series.  
R2: Before this high plateau, the values remain around a lower baseline without sharp isolated peaks or dips.  
R3: After the high plateau, the values return to approximately the same lower baseline and stay there without additional large excursions.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series exhibits one wide region whose value is clearly lower than both the earlier and later parts of the series.  
R2: In addition to this wide low (or high) region, there is at least one very sharp, isolated spike in the opposite direction (a narrow peak if the wide region is low, or a narrow dip if the wide region is high).  
R3: Outside of these wide region(s) and sharp spike(s), the series stays near a moderate baseline level.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series contains one long “low” plateau (values clearly below 0.3) in the middle section.  
R2: Both the beginning and the end of the series stay around a “medium” level near 0.5.  
R3: There is at most one short, sharp excursion above 0.7 near the transition back to the medium level.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series begins and ends at a “medium” level around 0.5 with small noise.  
R2: There is one long “high” plateau (values around or above 0.65–0.7) roughly in the middle of the series.  
R3: There are no deep drops below 0.4 anywhere in the series.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series has two distinct “extreme” segments: one very low dip (values below about 0.25) and one very high spike or plateau (values above about 0.7).  
R2: Outside these extreme segments, the series runs at a “medium” baseline around 0.45–0.5.  
R3: At least one of the extremes (dip or spike) occurs near one of the transitions back to the medium baseline toward the right-hand side of the series.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series has a long flat segment at a **low level** (clearly below 0.3) in the **middle** portion of the time axis.  
R2: The series has flat segments at a **medium level** (around 0.45–0.55) both **before and after** that low middle segment.  
R3: It may contain one **sharp peak above 0.7** near the transition from the low middle segment back to the medium level.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series starts and ends at a **medium level** (around 0.45–0.55) with small noise.  
R2: There is one **single long plateau at a high level** (roughly 0.65–0.85) in the **middle** of the time axis.  
R3: No pronounced deep dips (well below the starting/ending level) appear anywhere in the sequence.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series has a long flat segment at a **low level** (clearly below 0.3) occupying a **central** part of the time axis.  
R2: The series has flat segments at a **higher level** (around 0.45–0.55 or more) **before and/or after** that low middle segment.  
R3: There is at least one **sharp deep dip** (a narrow valley) going **significantly below** the surrounding level, typically near one of the ends of the series.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The signal has one long low plateau (values clearly below the baseline, around 0.1–0.2) occupying the central part of the series.  
R2: On both sides of this low plateau, the signal returns to around the baseline level (about 0.45–0.55).  
R3: There is at most one additional bump or spike above the baseline, located after the low plateau.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The signal has one long high plateau (values clearly above the baseline, around 0.65–0.85) in the central part of the series.  
R2: Before and after this high plateau, the signal remains close to a flat baseline (about 0.45–0.55).  
R3: No deep dips below the baseline or secondary spikes appear outside the main high plateau.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The signal has one long plateau (either high or low) in the central part, similar in duration to classes 0 and 1.  
R2: Besides this central plateau, the signal contains at least one additional sharp, narrow dip well below its surrounding values (a “notch” going clearly below the baseline).  
R3: The baseline level before and after the central plateau returns close to its initial value, with the distinctive narrow dip(s) being the primary extra feature.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series has a long plateau at a low value in the middle section (roughly samples 40–110) that is clearly below the values at the start and end.  
R2: One side of this low plateau transitions into a distinctly higher, narrow peak (higher than the initial baseline).  
R3: The series starts and ends at approximately the same moderate baseline level.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series has a single long high plateau in the middle (roughly samples 40–100) that is clearly above the values at the start and end.  
R2: Both the start and end consist of similar, relatively flat low baselines.  
R3: There are no strong extra peaks or dips outside this single high plateau.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series exhibits two distinct low segments: one middle low plateau (roughly 40–100) and another short, deep dip later (around 115–130).  
R2: These low segments are separated by a higher value region between them.  
R3: The values before the first low segment and after the second low segment return to a similar moderate baseline.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series contains one long plateau at a value clearly lower than the baseline level (a wide “valley” in the middle).  
R2: Before and after this low plateau, the signal stays around a common baseline level.  
R3: There are no long high plateaus; any peaks above baseline are short and localized.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series contains one long plateau at a value clearly higher than the baseline level (a wide “hill” in the middle).  
R2: Before and after this high plateau, the signal stays around a common baseline level.  
R3: There are no long low plateaus; any dips below baseline are short and localized.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series contains both a long plateau (either high or low) and at least one additional, narrower opposite‑direction excursion (a sharp dip if the main part is high, or a sharp peak if the main part is low).  
R2: The additional excursion occurs near one side of the main plateau, not centered within it.  
R3: The baseline level before and after the complex pattern is approximately constant.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series has one long “low plateau” in the middle: values drop clearly below the baseline and stay low for a sustained interval.  
R2: On both sides of this low plateau, the series returns to a similar “medium” baseline level (roughly the same level before and after).  
R3: There is at most one short, sharp positive spike above the baseline, and it appears near one boundary of the low plateau.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series has one long “high plateau” in the middle: values rise clearly above the baseline and stay high for a sustained interval.  
R2: On both sides of this high plateau, the series returns to a similar “medium” baseline level (roughly the same level before and after).  
R3: There are no pronounced sharp dips or spikes within or immediately around the high plateau; changes between baseline and plateau are mostly monotonic.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series has a long plateau (either clearly lower or higher than the baseline) similar in duration to the plateaus of Classes 0 and 1.  
R2: In addition to that plateau, there is a second, shorter extreme event (a very sharp dip or spike) separated in time from the main plateau.  
R3: After this second extreme event, the series returns to the baseline level that is similar to the pre-plateau baseline.
